review_body_text: Corporate style with multiple hierarchy, you get to survive if you have a strong mentality to cope stress. Stability is there just that need to work hard and sometimes luck.
Most people here are around your age so you’ll definitely be able to get along with everyone there. High salary, especially for fresh graduates who intend to work here

review_title: Productive and good place to learn
review_body_text: Good place and suitable for a starter or fresh graduate. Supportive colleagues as they always show the guidance towards the new employees. Able to learn a lot of valuable knowledges of how bank operations works on daily basis.

Stress and rigid working environment without system and computers upgrade. Software are outdated, payment system is slow and 90s era. Management level is rigid and has no one to one communication. This

Operation Team negative: Lack of staff lead to task overloaded, will be under pressure a lot. Need to cover the task for the colleague that take leave. Need to do a lot of OT but intern/trainee did not get paid OT. positive: The allowance was good. The colleagues are friendly. The environment was okay. Strategic location. If finish work early, can go shopping at Jalan Tar.

review_title: The worst place and management I've ever experienced
review_body_text: What is the best part of working at the company? Very toxic management style. Worst place I've ever worked at What is the most stressful part about working at the company? The job itself is stressful (the flow, the calls, the volume). The management doesn't help, but adds more stress What is the work environment and culture like at the company? Toxic, bad, negative. Simply appalling

Wished I check glassdoor reviews before joining. Colleagues are nice, some team leaders aren't helpful at all and will look down on you if you don't know certain things. Management doesn't care about you.

good colleagues, good environment and good management. No toxic and eventho you were overwhelmed with heavy workloads, but its okay. The team really supportive and helpful especially when youre having trouble/problem in your work. You can learn a lot from the team. I miss my teamates so much.
Overall ok, management ok, benefits ok, paid on time , supportive team, good bank, good allowance, good salary, good benefits, good system, treat us fairly ,

review_title: Fast paced but try to connect with competent seniors/colleagues
review_body_text: 1. You need to have communication skills including negotiation, patience, and listening skills to assist the customers with their inquiries outstandingly. Customers are generally normal and sane. Regardless they might rant because they are having difficulties. Just assist, advise, and resolve. 2. Systems are numerous. Getting used to its interface. Your daily tasks may help you to improve how to utilize them. Also, please give ample time to catch up and get used to the systems even though OIC might not assist you many times. Some OICs did good things when they willingly shared notes about raising the service request. You just need to refer. 3. The 1-month training class might not be enough, the notes you can't gather from outside of class. So for those who want to revise, please jot down clearly inside your notebook. Once you hit the floor, you only can refer to their Infohub system or your simplified notes. You try to focus on the necessary topic first. Ask your trainer the most common inquiries about EACH topic. Because trust me, you can't remember 100% from class to call floor later on. 3. OICs might be helping you but they will expect you to work independently, and catch up so fast. Sometimes, they might get irritated when you ask twice because they think they already explained thousands of times. Perhaps the same questions also come from your colleagues. So for those who are not familiar with banking terms/products, if your background is not related to banking/financials, you have to learn yourself. This one is supposed to be okay, but if you need them and they don't entertain willingly, definitely that will let you down. 4. Systems are fine except I only encounter difficulties with R*W**, it needs to be redesigned to a friendlier interface at least for those agents. I think it's too chaotic and tedious. Just need to make it readable so that easier to answer the enquiries instead of arranging a callback because you usually need more time to extract the info. 5. No work-life balance, kindly expect this as you need to perform follow-ups. But, as recommended by OICs/seniors, try to multitask by submitting the SR while talking with customers. Either listen to rants and do your job, or just put them on hold to submit the SR, both have been practiced there. You can choose to claim OT as well at the end of the shift to submit the SR but extra works that you have to get validation from your OICs or TM. Despite this being normal, they needed to check first. 6. I realized that OICs might have different understandings. So well equipped yourself with knowledge, and practiced to rely on many OICs/TMs for the same inquiries. You will notice to get different answers. 7. The job is fun, especially for those who like to talk to people and assist them. I wanted to stay but I felt disappointed for a couple of reasons, which made me resign. But rest assured, if you have REALLY good support and fun colleagues, you actually can survive.
pros_text: Benefits/Salary for fresh graduates/ OT claims

review_body_text: Low salary good colleagues manager is not professional and experienced, need to find the solution and solve the problem by yourself. Unclear commission calculate

Good compensation in term of flexi care which you can claim for flight, sport items, hobby class ++. Fun to mix around with other departments as everyone is in an open office. But, - paperwork overload - learned nothing - less guidance - No standard guidelines can follow (not sure whether is the manager itself problem). - Slow operating system. - When come to salary/OT/claim/commission, leader always try to deceive(always need to further find out with HR).

good experience and trained to become good customer services. They trained you to become professional in handling customer Good experience in banking system Friends are very friendly Making new friends and helpful friends

review_title: Productive and good for self advancement
review_body_text: UOB Customer Service Representative is a career that will help you prepare yourself with skills such as time management skills, multitasking, productivity management and you grow and stretch to go above and beyond to put a smile on customer’s face.
Learned a lot of skills. Very helpful managers that guides me in any way possible. Made many amazing friends . Pretty straightforward .
Entitled to COVID-19 test claim High internship allowance compared to other companies Superior & colleagues willing to teach & help intern Flexible working hour
You need to be good in multitasking because everything is going to be fast, you should learn to look for the solution by yourself in order for you to learn it.

review_title: They don’t care about your skills
review_body_text: They don’t really cater the roles to your skillset, and see you as contract staff despite calling the programme graduate trainee. Waste of time coming here. They don’t value talents at all.
One of the good place to start as a fresh grad. need to have good communication skills. good starting salary. Good company culture. Friendly team and nice colleague

the vibes and surrouding are goods for those who are not that kind of oldschool generation. very flex and fun workplace as u can work from anyplace in the office building. would recommend for those who seeking to starting a career in banking
